title: routes: facts/targets/where-selectors/sender
manager: Routes — `on fact -> target` — the declarative wiring that says which machine's outputs feed which machine's inputs, with `where` member-selectors to target subsets, observation sugar (enters/exits/takes), and `sender` as context. Routes are how a system is wired without imperative glue: you declare the information flow and the dispatcher makes it happen deterministically, so the wiring reads as a spec rather than a pile of event handlers.
cs: routes `on fact -> target delivery`; observation sugar enters/exits/takes; `where` member selectors; `sender` context only; the declarative wiring layer.
